title: Employment contracts

meta.description: Employment contracts in Australia — what they are, what they must include, and how they interact with awards and the NES.

headings.text: What a contract can and cannot do
Interaction with awards, agreements and NES

content:
An employment contract is an agreement between an employer and employee that sets out the terms and conditions of employment.

Written vs verbal contracts:
Employment contracts can be written, verbal, or a combination. There is no legal requirement in Australia for an employment contract to be in writing. However, written contracts provide clarity and are strongly recommended.

When there is no written contract:
- The employee's terms and conditions are still governed by the applicable Modern Award, enterprise agreement (if one exists), and the National Employment Standards (NES)
- The NES and applicable award set minimum entitlements that apply regardless of whether a written contract exists
- Verbal agreements or conduct can still form a legally binding employment contract

What a contract can and cannot do:
A contract CAN provide:
- Higher rates of pay than the award minimum
- Additional benefits (company car, extra leave, bonuses)
- Specific duties and responsibilities
- Flexibility in how work is performed

A contract CANNOT:
- Provide less than the minimum entitlements in the NES
- Provide less than the applicable Modern Award or enterprise agreement minimum rates
- Override or exclude NES entitlements (terms that do so are void and unenforceable)
- Include pay secrecy clauses that prevent employees from disclosing their pay to others (since 7 December 2022, pay secrecy terms in contracts cannot be enforced in most circumstances)

Interaction with awards, the NES and agreements:
Employment contracts sit within a hierarchy:
1. National Employment Standards (NES) — minimum floor — cannot be excluded
2. Modern Award or Enterprise Agreement — minimum rates and conditions
3. Employment Contract — can only improve on the above, not diminish

If a contract term conflicts with the NES or an applicable award, the NES/award prevails to the extent of the inconsistency.

Fixed-term contracts:
Since 6 December 2023, limitations apply to fixed-term contracts:
- A fixed-term contract generally cannot be for more than 2 years (including renewals)
- Contracts cannot be renewed or extended beyond the 2-year limit in most circumstances
- Employers must provide employees with a Fixed Term Contract Information Statement before or as soon as practicable after entering a fixed-term contract

Pay secrecy:
Since 7 December 2022, employers cannot include enforceable pay secrecy clauses in contracts. Employees have the right to disclose (or not disclose) their pay to others.
tables: No — NES is a minimum floor
No — cannot go below award minimum

key_facts.minimum_floor: NES + applicable Modern Award or Enterprise Agreement
